final_recommendation.text:
Based on your personal data, the best course of action for you is to not only invest in getting quality sleep but also increase the number of lightly active minutes in your day. Here is a detailed plan to follow: 

 1. Aim for 8 hours of sleep each night by setting a consistent sleep schedule. Go to bed at the same time each night and get up at the same time each morning, including on the weekends. 

 2.Incorporate more light physical activities into your daily routine. Try to take a 5-minute walking break every hour, or spend at least 30 minutes each day being lightly active, such as brisk walking or leisure cycling. 

 3. Work on reducing sedentary activities gradually. Try to stand or move around for a few minutes every hour. Swap some TV or computer time for something active or get up to stretch or move around during commercials or breaks. Apply these changes daily and you should see a gradual improvement in your stress levels.
final_recommendation.explanation: This recommendation is tailored to you based on your estimated causal effects and wearable data. Data suggests that sleep is the key factor affecting your stress, and our recommendation is grounded in the causation effect found in your data showing that better sleep patterns could help improve your stress scores. Furthermore, lightly increasing your active minutes has a positive effect on your stress score and will strengthen your stress management routine. Lastly, reducing your sedentary minutes is also shown to positively impact your stress level hence forming the third pillar of this recommendation.
